english: pf. °tams ('thams), 1. to seize, to lay hold of, to grasp, to take a firm hold of, esp. with the teeth (dogs), or the jaws (serpents W.); to sting (of bees W.); to embrace, *rkań-pa °tám-cè* (rkang pa 'tham ce) W. to put one's arms around a person's feet, as a supplicant; to grasp intellectually, to comprehend (?) Glr. - 2. to gnash, so one's teeth; to shut closely, k'a (kha) one's mouth, frq. - 3. to join, unite (vb. n.), grógs-su (grogs su), grógs-por (grogs por) Stg., in friendship, byá-bar (bya bar) in an act, an undertaking Dzl.

Lex. = mkrėgs-pa (mkregs pa), hard, solid; bag-čágs rgyúd-la °tas (bag chags rgyud la 'thas) prob.: inordinate desire has taken a firm hold of your minds; sra-°tás (sra 'thas) Sch. strong, robust, sinewy; a-°tas-te (a 'thas te), and a-°tas-kyi bag-čágs (a 'thas kyi bag chags) Pth.?

1. vb. n., pf. °tigs ('thigs) to drop, to fall in drops, to drop from, k'rag-ma °tigs-par (khrag ma 'thigs par) Lt. without any blood drop-ping out. - 2. vb. a., pf. btigs (btigs), fut. btig (btig) to cause to fall in drops, to instil etc.
Cs. a term of blame or abuse; Lexx.
a cover, covering; °tíbs-°og tsud ('thibs 'og tshud)? S.g.

pf. tibs (thibs) and gtibs (gtibs) (cf. rtibs-pa (rtibs pa)), to gather, of clouds, storms; ná-bun bźiń-du °tíbs-par gyúr-to (na bun bzhin du 'thibs par gyur to) (all the Buddhas) came drawing nearer like clouds of mist Glr.; to condensate, vb. n. ljon-śiń tams-cád dgá-bai tsál-du °tibs (ljon shing thams cad dga' ba'i tshal du 'thibs) all the trees afford a delightful shade Glr.; byiń °tibs (bying 'thibs) drowsiness overcomes me; po. and fig. to grow dark or dim, śés-pa (shes pa) con-sciousness Med. - °tíbs-po ('thibs po) dark, close, dense.

1. adj. v. tu-ba (thu ba). - 2. vb., also °tún-pa ('thun pa), pf. °tus ('thus), btus (btus), fut. btu (btu), imp. tus (thus), btu (btu) (Cs.), to gather, collect, pick up, śiń (shing), mè-tog (me tog), frq.; tus-mí (thus mi) an assemblage of men, council, Cs.

pf. °tuńs ('thungs) (Cs. also btuńs (btungs) I have drunk out), (fut. btuń (btung) Cs.), imp. °tuń ('thung), (Cs. also btuń (btung) drink out!), W. *tuń-cè* (thung ce), to drink, frq.; to suck, to smoke (tobacco), to eat (soup); to be soaked, drenched (cloth) Dzl.; ńóms-pa °túń-ba (ngoms pa 'thung ba) to drink one's fill Dzl.; żo-°tuńs (zho 'thungs), <o-°tuńs ('o 'thungs) suckling baby; żo-°tuń (zho 'thung) dus-na (dus na) during the time of giving suck Med.; °tuńs-pa tsám-gyis ('thungs pa tsam gyis) immediately after drinking Thgy.; °tuńs-so ('thungs so) they were engaged in drinking Glr.; °tuń-du ruń-ba ('thung du rung ba), W. *tuń-cóg* (thung cog), drink-able; btúń-ba (btung ba) sbst. drink, beverage, bzá-ba dań btúń-ba (bza' ba dang btung ba), bza-btúń (bza' btung) (W. *zabtúń* (za btung)) meat and drink, frq.; btúń-ču (btung chu) water for drinking Mil. -

adj. and abstr. sbst., °túg-po ('thug po) adj, thick, mta-°túg (mtha' 'thug) thicker toward the margin or edge Mńg.; gen. of woven stuffs, opp. to sráb-pa (srab pa); srab-°túg (srab 'thug) 1. thin and thick, 2. thickness relatively; also consistency, of liquids, opp to slá-ba (sla ba) Med.; dense, nags (nags), frq.; sound, heavy, gnyid °túg-po (gnyid 'thug po) a sound sleep; strong, bag-čágs °túg-po (bag chags 'thug po) a strong inclination Mil.

to make longer by adding a piece, to piece out, to prolong, pu-dúń (phu dung) W. a sleeve; skyé-ba °túd mi dgos (skye ba 'thud mi dgos) he has no need of adding a re-birth, a new period of life Pth.; °túd-ma ('thud ma) 1. addition, prolon-gation, *sróg-gi túd-ma táń-cè* (srog gi thud ma tang ce) W. prolong-ing life (by medicine, careful nursing). - 2. aid, assistance, subsidy, e.g. to a needy betrothed couple; also a gift of honour, a present, offered to a departing benefactor or respected Lama W.; dmag-°túd (dmag 'thud) sub-sidies; auxiliary troops. - 3. help, assistance in general.
gatherer, śiń-°tún (shing 'thun) a gatherer of wood, rtsa-°tún (rtsa 'thun) of grass.
pf. °tubs ('thubs), fut. gtub (gtub), imp. °tub ('thub), btub (btub), W. *túb-cè* (thub ce), to cut into pieces, v. gtub-pa (gtub pa).
pf. °tums ('thums), btums (btums), fut. btum (btum), imp. °tum ('thum), btum (btum), W. *túm-cè* (thum ce), to cover or lay over, to put over, to <
